sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section I, Histaklut Penimit 7

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section I, Histaklut Penimit 7:1
items.html:
Although only the fourth phase was restricted, the Light departed from the first three phases as well.
It has already been clarified that the middle point — the secret of the all-inclusive point of the thought of creation, the secret of the desire to enjoy in it — adorned itself to equalize its form with the Emanator more fully. Although from the side of the Emanator there is, in all His power, no disparity of form in it whatsoever, nevertheless the point of the desire sensed in this something like an indirect extension from His essence — in the manner of the above parable of the rich man; see there. Therefore it diminished its desire from the last phase, which is the utmost greatness of the desire to enjoy, in order to add adhesion by way of direct extension from His essence, as written above. Then the Light emptied out of the entire discernment of place — that is, from all four degrees that exist in the place. For although it diminished its desire only from the fourth phase, it is of the spiritual nature that it is not divided into parts.

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section I, Histaklut Penimit 7:2
items.html:
Afterwards He again extended a line of Light over the first three phases, and the fourth phase remained a vacant space.
After this, the Light of Ein Sof extended once more into the place that had been emptied — but it did not fill the place in all its four phases, only over three phases, in accordance with the desire of the point of the restriction. Thus the middle point that was restricted remained hollow and empty, for the Light shone only up to the fourth phase — up to, but not including — and there the Light of Ein Sof ceased. The matter of the inclusion of the phases in one another, which applies in the upper worlds, will be clarified below; by it you will understand that the four phases are included in one another in such a way that even in the fourth phase itself all four phases exist as well. It follows that even within the fourth phase, the Light of Ein Sof reached its first three phases; only the last phase within the fourth phase remained, it alone, empty and without Light. Remember this.
